description_long: This is a tall, elegant ceramic vase with a flared lip and a wide, stable base. The body of the vase gently tapers from the base upward, then expands outward before narrowing again at the neck. A decorative band featuring a stylized wave or scroll motif encircles the upper portion of the vase, adding visual interest and a classical touch. The surface is finished in a gradient of warm ochre and golden brown tones, giving it a sun-kissed, earthenware appearance. The vase appears to be hollow, suitable for holding flowers or decorative arrangements, and its smooth, hard surface suggests it is made from glazed ceramic. The overall design is refined and would complement both outdoor urban plazas and indoor public spaces.
description: A tall, ochre-toned ceramic vase with a flared lip, wide base, and decorative scroll motif around the upper body. Its smooth, classical form and warm gradient finish make it a striking decorative piece.
description_view_0: The vase is seen at an angle, highlighting the flared lip and the decorative scroll motif near the top, with the gradient color clearly visible.
description_view_1: A direct top-down view shows the circular opening and the symmetry of the flared rim, with the scroll motif partially visible around the neck.
description_view_2: Another angled view similar to view 0, but from the opposite side, again emphasizing the flared lip and the smooth, tapering body.
description_view_3: A straight-on side view reveals the full height and elegant taper of the vase, with the scroll motif and gradient color most clearly displayed.
